response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable software generalist with roughly 20 years of engineering experience and some recent AI-adjacent tooling work, but the role demands a recognized AI thought leader operating at director-level strategic scope. His AI exposure is confined to a six-month staff role in 2025 involving MCP code generators and agentic scaffolding — valuable, but far from the 'demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale' the role requires. There is no evidence of executive advisory relationships, enterprise customer engagement, or the kind of external recognition in AI that a Principal Engineer at this level is expected to bring. The educational profile (two bachelor's degrees, no advanced AI degree) and current career gap further widen the gap to the preferred candidate profile.
strengths: ~20 years of hands-on engineering breadth spanning web, mobile, embedded, infrastructure, and data-intensive systems
Entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective, held staff roles at multiple early-stage companies, shipped real open-source products
Recent AI-agent infrastructure exposure including MCP framework development and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and very recent — no recognized AI research, published work, or scaled AI product ownership that would credibly position him as a strategic AI advisor

No demonstrated director-level influence: career shows strong individual contributor output but no executive advisory relationships, enterprise customer engagement, or documented thought leadership beyond local workshops and one conference talk
Educational profile (BS Computer Science, BS Fine Arts) with no advanced degree in AI or a related technical field; role strongly prefers Master's or PhD in an AI-adjacent discipline
